home.name: Shota Sato
home.role: System Engineer · システムエンジニア
home.intro: Transitioned from diagnostic radiographer to software engineer. Focused on backend systems and cloud infrastructure, delivering reliable solutions.

about.label: About
about.title: Introduction
about.text1: I spent 4 years as a diagnostic radiographer at an orthopedic clinic, honing my image interpretation skills. Sharing knowledge through a blog and redesigning my workplace website sparked my interest in programming.
about.text2: I now focus on backend and cloud development, while also building frontend experiences with React and Three.js.

projects.label: Projects
projects.title: Projects
projects.desc: A selection of products I've built solo and with teams.

skills.label: Skills
skills.title: Skill Set
skills.desc: Technologies I've acquired through professional work and personal projects.

experience.label: Experience
experience.title: Career
experience.desc: After years in healthcare, I developed a passion for automation and systems — leading me to transition into software engineering with a focus on web development.

services.label: Services
services.title: Services
services.desc: I accept commissions for web development and system engineering — from small tools to full company websites.

corporate.title: Corporate Website / Homepage
corporate.desc: Homepage creation for businesses, shops, and sole proprietors.

wordpress.title: WordPress Site
wordpress.desc: Build an easy-to-update WordPress site tailored to your needs.

lp.title: Landing Page
lp.desc: LP creation for services, products, recruiting, and events.

infra.title: Hosting & Operations Support
infra.desc: Proposing fast, maintainable hosting environments leveraging AWS.

tools.title: Business Automation Tools
tools.desc: Build small tools and apps to streamline daily operations.

services.ctaText: Whether you have a specific request or just want to explore options — I'm happy to hear about your needs and budget, and propose the best approach.
services.ctaNote: Prices listed are estimates. Final cost depends on scope, features, and requirements.

contact.formValue: Please contact me via the form.

aboutPage.label: About
aboutPage.title: Profile
aboutPage.name: Shota Sato
aboutPage.p1: I've been working at an orthopedic clinic as a diagnostic radiographer for 5 years. I've focused on image interpretation — aiming to be a technician whose readings surpass those of physicians.
aboutPage.p2: Sharing expertise through a blog and redesigning my workplace website sparked my interest in programming. I've now been self-teaching for about a year.
aboutPage.p3: Recently, I've been building a web service using Laravel and Vue.js with a small team of friends.

skillfarm.description: Skill-sharing web service built with Laravel + Vue.js. Led design and implementation as part of a team.

blog.description: Technical blog sharing specialized medical imaging knowledge. Built on WordPress with SEO-focused structure.

applicationManager.description: Interactive portfolio site featuring a Three.js particle background.

0.title: System Engineer
0.description: Designing and developing web applications. Focused on backend and cloud infrastructure in product development.

1.title: Engineering Start
1.description: Started learning development while still working as a radiographer. Proposed and built the clinic's website, and created personal blog and portfolio sites.

2.title: Medical Background
2.description: Joined an orthopedic clinic as a new graduate. Built hands-on experience as a diagnostic radiographer in a clinical setting.

skillfarm.label: Project
skillfarm.title: Skillfarm
skillfarm.aboutDesc: A matching app connecting people who want to learn programming with those studying Japanese or English. Features a custom CEFR-based proficiency system for smooth cross-language communication.
skillfarm.techDesc: Laravel 8 / Inertia.js / Vue.js / AWS (EC2) / Twilio / Pusher / SQLite / Git / Figma
skillfarm.roleDesc: Led the project as project manager. Primarily responsible for backend, Vue.js frontend, and technical articles.

0.desc: Recruited 2 non-engineer friends on 2021/11/28 to form a grassroots dev team — frontend, backend (my main role), and marketing.

1.desc: 2 more members joined, growing to a 5-person team with a new infrastructure division.

2.desc: Marketing lead left due to a busy day job. Twitter efforts underperformed — impressions were about 1/10 of average.

3.desc: Switching hashtag strategy led to a dramatic jump in impressions.

4.desc: Alongside comic uploads, began posting technical articles on the stalled blog.

5.desc: Passed Google AdSense review on the 5th article.

6.desc: App completed in local environment on 2022/04/14.

blog.label: Project
blog.title: Radiographer Blog
blog.aboutDesc: A site focused on image interpretation — analyzing medical images to detect abnormalities. Built from near-zero knowledge with 3 years of dedicated study, primarily interpreting real-world clinical cases found online.
blog.techDesc: WordPress / jQuery / JavaScript / HTML5 / SCSS / MySQL / mixhost

0.flag: Built Blog as Web Dev Practice
0.desc: Struggled through local-to-production deployment and contact form setup, but completed the site.

1.desc: Likely due to thin content, poor SEO, and being built on a subdirectory.

2.desc: Migrated the site with an SEO-focused redesign.

3.desc: Submitted and passed the Google AdSense review.

4.desc: Shut down the blog after transitioning to software engineering.

appManager.label: Project
appManager.title: Application Manager
appManager.overviewDesc: A serverless web application for registering and managing applicant information. Users can apply to listings after creating an account, with all submissions centrally managed by an admin. Automated email notifications are sent to admins upon new applications.
appManager.techDesc: AWS Lambda / API Gateway / DynamoDB / Terraform / Python / JavaScript / Docker / SAM

0.desc: Defined use cases for the application management system and designed operation flows for both users and admins. Structured the overall architecture around a serverless approach.

1.desc: Built user registration and login with session management. Set up a DynamoDB-based data management layer.

2.desc: Implemented user-facing application submission and admin-facing listing/search features. Designed screens and API integration around real business workflows.

3.desc: Implemented admin email notifications on new applications. Designed event-driven processing in a serverless Lambda environment.

4.desc: Managed all AWS resources with Terraform for reproducible infrastructure. Streamlined deployment and operations through automation.
